extra.reasoning:
The monotonicity property (setting palpitations=true never lowers acuity) is VIOLATED.

palpitations appears in exactly one rule: interaction_drop (priority 100), which sets acuity := self_care (the lowest enum value) and requires pallor ∧ vision_loss ∧ fever ∧ sweating ∧ palpitations. Because it has the highest priority by far (100 vs. the priority-3 emergent rules), whenever it fires it dominates all other rules.

Counterexample pair (all other inputs held at: pallor=T, vision_loss=T, fever=T, sweating=T, focal_weakness=T):
- palpitations=FALSE: interaction_drop does not fire (palpitations missing). The active rules include raise_sweating, raise_focal_weakness, and raise_vision_loss (each priority 3 → emergent), plus raise_fever/raise_pallor (priority 2 → urgent). Highest priority among firing rules is 3, all agreeing on emergent. Result: acuity = emergent.
- palpitations=TRUE: now interaction_drop fires (all five of pallor, vision_loss, fever, sweating, palpitations are true). With priority 100 it beats every priority-3 emergent rule. Result: acuity = self_care.

Toggling palpitations from false to true lowered acuity from emergent (highest) to self_care (lowest), directly violating the property. The witness_assignment gives the palpitations=true endpoint that produces the lowered acuity; the baseline is the identical assignment with palpitations=false.
